Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint _1087231-01
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHARLIE W. YU OF THE HEDANI, CHOY, SPALDING LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-778-0800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KIRK M. OLSON OF THE McMLUSKEY & MONTGOMERY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-310-396-3411 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS TIE LIU, T J CONSTRUCTION INC., INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INSURANCE COMPANY, SURETEC INSURANCE COMPANY AND BUSINESS ALLIANCE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Plaintiffs have failed to plead sufficient facts to support a showing of malice, oppression, or fraud on the part of Defendants. Plaintiffs also failed to plead the existence of a contract term of specific statute to recover attorney's fees. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHARLIE W. YU OF THE HEDANI, CHOY, SPALDING LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-778-0800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KIRK M. OLSON OF THE McMLUSKEY & MONTGOMERY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-310-396-3411 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS TIE LIU, T J CONSTRUCTION INC., INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INSURANCE COMPANY, SURETEC INSURANCE COMPANY and BUSINESS ALLIANCE INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED IN PART. Plaintiffs do not state with specificity their causes of action for fraud and elder abuse. (See Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 216, held: " '[A]llegations of fraud involve a serious attack on character, and fairness to the defendant demands that he should receive the fullest possible details of the charge in order to prepare his defense.' ") Plaintiffs sufficiently plead their causes of action for negligence, breach of contract and unfair business practices. Plaintiff was given 10 days leave to amend. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS TIE LIU, T J CONSTRUCTION INC., AND SURETEC INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to all causes of action and the motion to strike is taken off calendar as moot. All claims are inadequately pled. While brevity is generally a good idea in pleadings, where, as here, brevity is at the cost of giving adequate notice to defendants of the basic facts that they need to defend against, brevity needs to give away to the demands of notice pleading. Order signed. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/HK)
